strategy: L3 Behaviour Proven is the current programme milestone; L4 Substrate Proven work is blocked until behavioural readiness is complete.

oldToNewMapping.rationale: L0 is retained as non-executable inventory confidence covering discovered capabilities, routes, workflows, providers, storage, events, ownership, and dependencies.
Successful proof command execution is now separated from contract confidence so no-crash evidence is not overstated.
L2 now means interface/input/output/permission/invariant contract exercise only; state transitions belong to L3.
L3 is expanded into the explicit behavioural closure target: before/after state, state diff, side effects, failure path, audit/metric/trace, tenant/security boundaries, and deterministic replay.
L4 remains real local substrate confidence but is now gated by complete L3 behavioural evidence.
L5 no longer means external sandbox alone; it requires L4 plus restart, timeout, retry, concurrency, recovery, backup/restore, degraded-mode, and failure-injection evidence.
L6 is now full foundation assurance: L5 plus tenancy, security, observability, operational recovery, lifecycle governance, and ownership.

newProofLevels.name: Discovery Proven
newProofLevels.purpose: inventory confidence only; no execution guarantees

newProofLevels.name: Executable Proven
newProofLevels.purpose: execution confidence only; no behavioural guarantees

newProofLevels.name: Behaviour Proven
newProofLevels.purpose: behavioural confidence and current programme closure target

newProofLevels.name: Substrate Proven
newProofLevels.purpose: implementation correctness confidence against real local substrate

newProofLevels.name: Foundation Proven
newProofLevels.purpose: USF primitives proven sufficient for production-grade services
